description: Polish Dashboard, Sidebar, Task views, Code panel, Settings, Onboarding, and Session views for a cohesive, stunning macOS experience using the expanded DesignConstants.
steps: STEP 1 - Polish TaskCard.swift and AgentCard.swift (Dashboard cards): (a) TaskCard: reduce hover scale from 1.02 to DesignConstants.hoverScale (1.015) for subtlety. Replace shadow values with DesignConstants.cardShadowRadius/cardShadowY. Use DesignConstants.cardCornerRadius instead of cornerRadiusLarge. Add a subtle border using separatorColor at cardBorderOpacity even when not hovered, for definition. The attention border should use .orange at 0.4 opacity. (b) AgentCard: apply the same hover scale, shadow, and corner radius changes as TaskCard for consistency. Both cards should look identical in visual weight and style.

STEP 2 - Polish DashboardView.swift, QuickActionsView.swift, ActivityFeed.swift, WorktreeAgentsList.swift: (a) DashboardView: use DesignConstants.spacingXL (24) for section spacing instead of hardcoded 16. Ensure the attention section border uses cardCornerRadius. (b) QuickActionsView: use DesignConstants.cardCornerRadius for the background, ensure button text is not too small (min 12pt is fine). Replace hardcoded padding values with DesignConstants spacing constants. (c) ActivityFeed: add subtle hover effect to activity rows (background highlight on hover). Use DesignConstants.badgeCornerRadius for the branch badge. Ensure the feed header uses consistent font weight. (d) WorktreeAgentsList: use DesignConstants.cardCornerRadius for the workspace group card background. Ensure TaskListRow hover effect uses DesignConstants.hoverScale or at minimum a consistent hover background.

STEP 3 - Polish SidebarView.swift and TaskRow.swift: (a) TaskRow: the status icon is .title2 (too large for a sidebar row) - reduce to .body or a 16pt system size for better proportion. The task name font is .headline - keep it but ensure it doesn't feel too heavy; if sidebar rows look too bold, consider .subheadline with .fontWeight(.medium) instead. The git branch badge and file changes badge should use DesignConstants.badgeCornerRadius and badgePadding consistently. Ensure vertical padding is comfortable but compact (8pt total, currently 4pt top + 4pt bottom which is fine). (b) SidebarView: the list already uses .listStyle(.sidebar) which is good. Ensure section headers have consistent styling.

STEP 4 - Polish NewTaskSheet.swift and TaskAgentView.swift: (a) NewTaskSheet: the sheet frame is hardcoded at 500x520/400. Ensure minimum dimensions are reasonable and the sheet can breathe. The footer buttons area: ensure Cancel and Create Task are well-spaced with Spacer between them. The 'Create Task' button should use .buttonStyle(.borderedProminent) which it already does - good. Ensure form sections have consistent spacing. (b) TaskAgentView: the task header bar: use DesignConstants.cardBackground for the background. Ensure the action buttons (Start, Pause, Resume, Complete, Cancel) have consistent sizing using .controlSize(.small) and appropriate button styles (.borderedProminent for primary actions like Start/Resume, .bordered for secondary actions like Pause/Complete, .plain with .foregroundColor(.red) for Cancel). The error banner: use DesignConstants.dangerSubtle for the background color. TaskPendingView: ensure the 'Start Task' button is not excessively large - .controlSize(.regular) instead of .large for better macOS proportions. TaskCompletedView: ensure the completion banner uses consistent corner radii and padding from DesignConstants.

STEP 5 - Polish CodePanel.swift, TerminalOutputView.swift, DiffView.swift, FilesChangedView.swift: (a) CodePanel: the segmented picker is fine. Ensure padding around it uses DesignConstants values. (b) TerminalOutputView: the terminal entry header uses hardcoded padding(8) and padding(6) - replace with DesignConstants.spacingSM and spacingXS. Ensure the terminal entry card uses DesignConstants.cardCornerRadius (through cornerRadiusMedium which is 8 - this is fine for code cards). The output text area: use consistent monospaced font sizing from DesignConstants. (c) DiffView: the file header already uses DesignConstants - good. Ensure the hunk header button uses consistent padding. (d) FilesChangedView: the file chip strip should use DesignConstants consistently. The chip cornerRadius is cornerRadiusSmall (4) - this is a bit sharp for chips; use cornerRadiusMedium (8) or a new chipCornerRadius of 6 for a more refined pill-like appearance. Add chipCornerRadius = 6 to DesignConstants if needed.

STEP 6 - Polish SettingsView.swift and GeneralSettingsView.swift: (a) SettingsView: the frame has minHeight 350, maxHeight 500. Increase to minWidth: 500, minHeight: 400, maxHeight: 550 for comfortable settings. (b) AgentSettingsView: the AgentConfigRow uses hardcoded frame(maxWidth: 250) for text fields - increase to 300 for more comfortable input. Ensure the delete/set-default buttons have consistent spacing. (c) AppearanceSettingsView: the theme picker and font size slider are fine with .formStyle(.grouped). No major changes needed. (d) GeneralSettingsView: ensure Browse buttons are consistently styled across all settings views.

STEP 7 - Polish OnboardingView.swift: (a) The onboarding window is 550x450 which is good. The progress indicator dots: increase from 8pt to 10pt for easier visibility. (b) The welcome step icon is 72pt which is good. Feature rows: ensure consistent icon width and spacing. (c) The configure CLI step: ensure the path validation indicator and Browse button are well-aligned. (d) The create workspace step: ensure the form fields have comfortable spacing. (e) The navigation buttons at the bottom: ensure the Continue button uses .buttonStyle(.borderedProminent) which it already does. The Back button could use a more subtle style. Overall the onboarding is already reasonably polished.

STEP 8 - Polish SessionHistoryView.swift, SessionRow.swift, SessionNameEditor.swift: (a) SessionRow: the resume button is already well-styled with .borderedProminent/.small. The session icon at .title2 is consistent. Ensure the message count badge uses DesignConstants.badgeCornerRadius. The preview text should have a subtle background or visual separation. (b) SessionNameEditor: ensure the dialog has comfortable minimum width (keep 300). The suggestion button should use a more visible style - perhaps add a subtle background badge around the suggestion text. (c) SessionHistoryView: ensure the empty state icon size is consistent with other empty states in the app (use 48pt consistently).

STEP 9 - Polish ErrorBanner.swift and remaining Components: (a) ErrorBanner: the gradient background is distinctive. Use DesignConstants.cornerRadiusMedium consistently. Ensure the retry button has adequate hit target. (b) StatusBadge: ensure the circle indicator is 8pt (it is). (c) TaskStatusBadge: consistent with StatusBadge. (d) Ensure all Workspace views (WorkspaceSettingsView, WorktreeSelector, WorkspaceAgentsView, NewWorkspaceSheet) use DesignConstants for any hardcoded corner radii, padding, and shadow values. These are mostly deprecated views but they should still look consistent if opened.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard cards (TaskCard, AgentCard) use identical visual weight with consistent corner radii, shadows, and hover effects from DesignConstants
Sidebar TaskRow uses proportionally sized icons (not oversized .title2)
All badges (git branch, file count, message count, status) use DesignConstants.badgeCornerRadius consistently
Task action buttons in TaskAgentView have consistent sizing and clear visual hierarchy (primary vs secondary actions)
Terminal entries and code panel elements use DesignConstants for all padding and corner radii
Settings window has comfortable minimum dimensions
Onboarding progress dots are comfortably sized
No hardcoded magic numbers for visual properties remain in any modified view
verification: Run 'swift build' and confirm it succeeds with exit code 0
Run 'swift test' and confirm all existing tests still pass
